content_markdown: Two bodies are brought into thermal contact with each other. No thermal energy transfer takes place between the bodies. It may be deduced therefore, that the bodies must have the same

diagram_metadata.description: No diagram provided in the image.

vibe_explanation: Thermal equilibrium is defined by the Zeroth Law of Thermodynamics. If two objects are in thermal contact and no net heat flows between them, they must be at the same temperature. Other properties like internal energy or heat capacity depend on the mass and nature of the substance, but temperature is the sole determinant for the direction of thermal energy transfer.
